body.title: [triage] Testing rack device — runs a builder's output (tests/builds) in an isolated container
body.intention: I intend that a rack device executes a builder's produced tests/builds inside an isolated container, so the Hex-hosted builder assistant's output can be run safely and its red->green verified without touching the host.
body.description:
**⚠ DUPLICATE — SUPERSEDED BY `T-tester-rackmount`, which is the canonical design.**

Both tickets describe ONE device: a rack device that runs a builder's tests/builds in an isolated
container and returns an independent verdict. `T-tester-rackmount` is the fuller design — it adds the
principle that makes the device worth building (**separation of powers: the builder never grades its own
work**) and it explicitly ABSORBS `D-containers-sandbox-device-2026-07-07` (the container is the tester's
execution environment, not a standalone device).

Keeping two open tickets for one device is how a design gets built twice, or half. Consolidated
2026-07-13. **Work `T-tester-rackmount`.**

Today's evidence for the device (nine host-shelled pytest runs saturating Hex's single inference slot; the
container's `--network=none` model being what makes a capacity lease unbypassable) is recorded on
`T-tester-rackmount`, not here.

--- ORIGINAL (preserved, CP2) ---
VISION / NEXT-PHASE (Akien 2026-07-08, while sprinting the inference router). North star: a builder assistant runs on Hex and escalates as needed (the inference router redesign is its routing brain), AND a TESTING RACK DEVICE with its own CONTAINER runs the builds/tests the builder produces. The container isolates execution (a builder's generated code runs sandboxed, not on the host); the device reports pass/fail + the red->green signal proof-on-close needs. This is the safe-execution partner to the builder assistant and a step toward the automated test factory (see project_automated_test_factory).

**Affected files:** TBD — new device under unseen_university/devices/<testing|examiner>/ following device.py/shim.py; container runtime wiring; likely consumes proof_emitter / the post-build reconciliation.
**Design rules:** device-shim-design-center; one-daemon-ground-loop; external-state-principle; no-sqlite.
**Scope boundary:** VISION capture only — not to be built until the inference router redesign ships and the shape is designed (needs its own /sorted). Investigate container runtime + isolation model first.
**Test plan:** TBD at design time — a build submitted to the device runs its tests in-container and returns a pass/fail + proof-shaped result.
**Depends on:** the inference router redesign (builder routing) + a design pass for the device shape.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Closed as DUPLICATE of T-tester-rackmount (the canonical, fuller design — adds separation-of-powers and absorbs D-containers-sandbox-device-2026-07-07). **Proof of a won't-do close is the DECISION** (CLAUDE.md: 'a won't-do close is not a proofless close: its proof is the DECISION'): two open tickets for one device is how a design gets built twice, or half. No work is dropped — the scope lives on in T-tester-rackmount, which carries today's new evidence.
